zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Thorpe於大約1395年出生在英國諾福爾可阿申維爾索普時，她的父親Edmund Thorpe54歲，母親Joan de Northwode38歲。她於1412年與Sir Phillip Tilney在英國英格蘭諾福爾可阿申維爾索普結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1436年11月10日在她的家鄉享年42歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可阿申維爾索普。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel de Thorpe于大约1395年出生在英国諾福爾可阿申維爾索普时，她的父亲Edmund Thorpe54岁，母亲Joan de Northwode38岁。她于1412年与Sir Phillip Tilney在英国英格兰諾福爾可阿申維爾索普结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1436年11月10日在她的家乡享年42岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可阿申維爾索普。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Thorpe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): from Middle English thorp(e) ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English thorp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from any of several places called Thorp(e), which are most frequent in Yorkshire, the Midlands, and East Anglia. Compare Throop .

variants.name: Trupp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Hermann, Reinhold.

English (Herefordshire and Monmouthshire): variant of   Thorpe  , or from a place so named, such as Thrupp (Berkshire, Gloucestershire, Oxfordshire), Thrupe (Somerset), Throop (Dorset, Hampshire, Somerset), and Throope (Wiltshire).
Altered form of German   Trapp  .

variants.name: Throop
meanings: English: from Middle English throp ‘village, hamlet, farmstead’ (Old Norse, Old English throp ‘secondary settlement’). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in such a settlement, or habitational, for someone from Throop in Hampshire, Throope in Wiltshire, Thrup in Oxfordshire, or any of the places called Thrupp in Berkshire, Gloucestershire, and Northamptonshire. Compare Thorpe .

variants.name: Tharpe
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Thorpe  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

variants.name: Thorp
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Thorpe  .
Scandinavian: variant of   Torp  .

variants.name: Troup
meanings: Scottish (eastern): habitational name from a place in the parish of Gamrie, near Banff. The placename is unexplained, perhaps a Pictish cognate of Welsh trwp ‘vessel, dish, basin’.
Possibly also English, a metathesized form of   Thorpe  .

variants.name: Tharp
meanings: English: variant of   Thorpe  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Thorpe was born about 1395, in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, her father, Edmund Thorpe, was 54 and her mother, Joan de Northwode, was 38. She married Sir Phillip Tilney in 1412, in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 10 November 1436, in her hometown, at the age of 42, and was buried in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Thorpe est née environ 1395, à Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Thorpe, avait 54 ans et sa mère, Joan de Northwode, avait 38 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Phillip Tilney en 1412, à Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 10 novembre 1436, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterrée à Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Thorpe um 1395 in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund Thorpe 54 und ihre Mutter Joan de Northwode 38. Sie hat 1412 in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Sir Phillip Tilney geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 10. November 1436 im Alter von 42 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Thorpe è nata il circa 1395 nel Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England, sua padre, Edmund Thorpe, aveva 54 anni e sua madre, Joan de Northwode, aveva 38 anni. Lei sposato Sir Phillip Tilney nel 1412, nel Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 10 novembre 1436, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 42 anni, e fu sepolta a Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Thorpeが約1395年にイギリスノーフォルクアシュウエルソルペで生まれた時，父Edmund Thorpeは54歳，母Joan de Northwodeは38歳であった。1412年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクアシュウエルソルペでSir Phillip Tilneyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1436年11月10日に故郷で42歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクアシュウエルソルペにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Thorpe(이)가 대략 1395년 영국 노르폴크 애스h웰소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Thorpe(은)는 54세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Northwode(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1412년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 애스h웰소에서 Sir Phillip Tilney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1436년 11월 10일 그녀의 고향에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 애스h웰소에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Thorpe nasceu aproximadamente 1395, em Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Thorpe, tinha 54 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Northwode, tinha 38 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Phillip Tilney em 1412, em Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 10 de novembro de 1436, em sua cidade natal, com 42 anos, e foi sepultada em Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Thorpe родилась около 1395 г. в местности ашвеллтаурп, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund Thorpe было 54 года, а матери, которую звали Joan de Northwode, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Phillip Tilney в 1412 г. в местности ашвеллтаурп, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 10 ноября 1436 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 42 лет и была похоронена в местности ашвеллтаурп, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel de Thorpe nació aproximadamente 1395, en Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund Thorpe, tenía 54 años y su mamá, Joan de Northwode, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Sir Phillip Tilney en 1412, en Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 10 de noviembre de 1436, en su pueblo, a la edad de 42 años, y fue sepultada en Ashwellthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
